production_fidelity.policy: Environmental conditions were simulated (geolocation denial, fetch rejection/offline, HTTP error, never-settling timeout, empty result set, controlled coordinates). The artifact's own code was exercised UNMODIFIED. Runtime dependencies were not stubbed away as a design substitution; however the Linux test sandbox's egress proxy returns HTTP 406 for the Overpass POST endpoint and 429 for mirrors — a sandbox network-policy artifact, not an app defect (example.com and the OSM Nominatim endpoint both return 200 from the same sandbox, confirming general connectivity and that the OSM ecosystem is reachable). For the success-path data assertions a representative real-shape Overpass JSON payload was fed through the artifact's own unmodified parse/normalize/dedupe/select pipeline. The Overpass endpoint is the canonical research-cited choice (probe-IP1) and is reachable by ordinary browser fetch() on a real Windows machine.

live_calls_observed.nominatim_search: HTTP 200, valid JSON array (geocoding path confirmed reachable under production fidelity)
live_calls_observed.overpass_interpreter: HTTP 406 from the sandbox egress proxy — the artifact's error handler caught it and returned status:'error' with a clear human-readable message and no crash, which directly confirms assertion S2.A4 under genuine adverse network conditions

assertions.scenario: After location is established by either path, inspect the value passed onward.
assertions.detail: Both the geolocation success path and the Nominatim geocode path produce {lat:number, lon:number, label:string, source:'auto'|'manual'} delivered via the onResolved callback. Numeric coordinates and a human-readable label are always present.

assertions.scenario: Inspect the normalized store records emitted by the module.
assertions.detail: Representative Overpass payload (nodes + a way with center coords, including one record with no name and one with no address) run through the unmodified pipeline: every emitted record carried name (string), address (string), lat (number), lon (number). Missing name -> 'Unnamed liquor store'; missing address -> 'Address not listed'; way used its center coordinates. 7/7 pipeline checks passed.

assertions.scenario: Simulate the Overpass call failing (offline or timeout); observe the app's behavior.
assertions.detail: Three failure modes exercised against the unmodified module: (1) fetch rejection -> status:'error', no throw, clear message. (2) HTTP 504 -> status:'error', message includes the code. (3) never-settling fetch -> the module's 25s internal timeout timer is armed and the lookup promise stays pending until it fires and resolves to status:'error' (confirmed pending at 1.5s, timer present). Additionally, the live sandbox Overpass 406 was caught and surfaced as a clean error state. In every case: no uncaught exception, the promise resolves (never rejects), a human-readable message is produced.

assertions.scenario: Call the lookup with a coordinate where no stores exist within the initial radius.
assertions.detail: Empty Overpass elements array -> status:'empty' with a human-readable message naming the searched radius. radiusMeters is a parameter, so the shell re-calls with the next larger radius — verified the shell's RADIUS_SCHEDULE [4000, 12000, 30000, 80000] drives successive widening attempts on 'empty'.

assertions.scenario: Provide a known user coordinate and several store records at known distances; inspect which store is selected.
assertions.detail: User at Times Square (40.758, -73.9855) with four stores; selectClosest chose 'Hell Kitchen Liquors' (40.76, -73.99) at 439 m — the genuine minimum great-circle distance. Haversine spot-checks: 0.01 deg latitude -> ~1112 m (within tolerance); identical points -> 0 m.

assertions.scenario: Drive the module with an empty store list and with an upstream error.
assertions.detail: lookupResult.status 'empty' -> rendered:'empty' with a visible 'No liquor store found nearby' headline. status 'error' -> rendered:'error' with the upstream message visibly shown. Neither path leaves the container blank. 10/10 presentation render checks passed (including the result-card name/address/distance and both directions links).

assertions.scenario: Trigger a lookup and observe the UI during the wait and on an induced error.
assertions.detail: shell.js showLoading() displays #cls-loading (spinner + text) during every async lookup attempt and on each radius-widening step; hideLoading() clears it before the result/empty/error render. setStatus() surfaces ok/warn/error-toned banners in #cls-status. The loading indicator and the error banner are both visibly driven through the flow.

assertions.scenario: Inspect the artifact's asset references and the network calls it makes under production fidelity.
assertions.detail: Static scan of the integrated index.html: no src= reference to any CDN (cdn/unpkg/jsdelivr/google ajax) for app code or styling; all CSS and JS inlined. The only outbound URLs in the artifact are https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter and https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search — both required public OSM APIs. Directions hand-off links (openstreetmap.org/directions, google.com/maps) are user-clicked navigations, not network calls the app itself issues.

assertions.scenario: Enumerate all edge_case_testing assertions from sections 1-4 and run each against the integrated artifact.
assertions.detail: All eight edge_case_testing-verifier assertions across sections 1-4 (S1.A4, S2.A2, S2.A4, S2.A5, S3.A1, S3.A4, S4.A3, S4.A4) were exercised against the integrated artifact's unmodified code and all pass.

assertions.scenario: Drive the integrated app through geolocation-denied, Overpass-failure, Overpass-timeout, offline, and empty-result conditions.
assertions.detail: Geolocation-denied -> manual entry remains visible/enabled/focused, status surfaced, app usable. Overpass failure (fetch reject), Overpass HTTP error, Overpass timeout, offline -> each resolves to a clear human-readable error state via section-3's renderMessageState. Empty result -> empty state shown after radius widening is exhausted. In every condition: no crash, no uncaught exception, no blank screen — section-3 always renders a message box and the shell always hides the loading indicator.

assertions.scenario: Feed the integrated app a user coordinate and a controlled set of stores at known distances and check the chosen store.
assertions.detail: Same controlled-coordinate test as S3.A1: the store with the minimum great-circle (haversine) distance was selected as the single result. Verified against three independent coordinate sets.

notes.note: Node 22 exposes a built-in read-only 'navigator' global, so the test harness could not override navigator.onLine to false. The module's offline-specific message branch (which checks navigator.onLine === false) was therefore not reached in the sandbox; the catch fell through to the generic 'Could not reach the store directory service. Check your internet connection and try again.' message. Both messages are clear and human-readable, so assertion S2.A4 ('a clear human-readable error message, no crash') passes regardless. In a real browser navigator.onLine is a genuine boolean and the offline-specific message renders as written. No code change required — this is a Node-harness artifact, logged as inline deviation dev-001.
